qa_pairs.question: How do I sign up for an Alliance account?
qa_pairs.answer: To sign up for an Alliance account, go to the register link at https://ccdb.alliancecan.ca/account_application.

qa_pairs.question: What is required to get an Alliance account?
qa_pairs.answer: To get an Alliance account, you will have to accept certain policies and agreements.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I read the Alliance policies and agreements?
qa_pairs.answer: You can read the Alliance policies on the Alliance website at https://alliancecan.ca/en/policies.

qa_pairs.question: How can I view the agreements I've accepted after my account has been approved?
qa_pairs.answer: Once your account has been approved, you can view the agreements you've accepted at any time on CCDB by navigating to 'My Account --> Agreements' at https://ccdb.alliancecan.ca/agreements/user_index.

qa_pairs.question: What are the two main types of roles when applying for an account?
qa_pairs.answer: The two main types of roles are sponsor roles, often referred to as Primary Investigators (PIs), and sponsored roles.

qa_pairs.question: Who can be a sponsor for an Alliance account?
qa_pairs.answer: Only faculty members can be sponsors.

qa_pairs.question: What should non-faculty administrators who lead research projects do when applying for an account?
qa_pairs.answer: Administrators who are not faculty but who lead research projects must contact accounts@tech.alliancecan.ca for appropriate accommodations.

qa_pairs.question: Who is eligible for faculty roles with the Alliance?
qa_pairs.answer: Faculty roles are only granted to faculty from Canadian post-secondary academic institutions who are eligible for CFI funding.

qa_pairs.question: How are sponsored roles categorized?
qa_pairs.answer: Sponsored roles fall into two groups: internal and external, depending on whether the applicant is part of the supervisor's local group or a collaborator from a different institution.

qa_pairs.question: What specific sponsored roles are available?
qa_pairs.answer: Available sponsored roles include undergraduate, masters, and doctoral students; postdoctoral fellows and researchers affiliated with the PI's institution (if paid by the PI's institution); non-research staff; and external collaborators.

qa_pairs.question: What is an external collaborator and what is a key requirement for them?
qa_pairs.answer: An external collaborator is anyone working with a PI's group whom the PI is willing to sponsor. External collaborators must specify an institution different from that of the PI.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find a comprehensive list of all user roles?
qa_pairs.answer: The full list of roles is available on the 'User roles to access resources and services of the Alliance Federation' page.

qa_pairs.question: What happens immediately after I submit my account request?
qa_pairs.answer: Immediately after submitting your account request, you will receive an email with a link to confirm the email address you provided.

qa_pairs.question: How is an Alliance account application approved?
qa_pairs.answer: If you are a principal investigator, your application will be approved by a Federation staff. Otherwise, it will need to be confirmed by your identified sponsor before being approved by a Federation staff.

qa_pairs.question: Who should I contact for help with questions not covered in the FAQ?
qa_pairs.answer: For any questions not covered in the FAQ, you should send an email to accounts@tech.alliancecan.ca.
